description: Adding trigger-based async balance maintenance to a payment system. The application reads balance from a read replica during checkout. Review consistency, atomicity, and correctness implications.
review_checklist: Identify read-after-write inconsistency risks across primary/replica
Verify atomicity of trigger relative to inserting transaction
Check whether dropped CHECK constraints can be re-validated
Evaluate trigger overhead on write hot path
Audit whether overdraft/race conditions are now possible

ground_truth.description: Trigger-based balance update creates a fundamental read-after-write hazard. Application reads balance from read replica (replication lag 50-2000ms); user just paid, balance not yet replicated → checkout shows old balance → user issues another payment → overdraft. Financial systems require synchronous balance reads from primary OR explicit version-check on the read path.

Trigger UPDATE on accounts is not idempotent under retries. If the calling transaction retries due to serialization conflict, the trigger fires twice and double-credits. Use idempotency via payment_id uniqueness or move balance computation out of the trigger into an explicit two-phase pattern.

Dropping the positive_balance CHECK constraint on a financial table eliminates the last line of defense against overdraft bugs. Constraint should be retained — re-add with NOT VALID and VALIDATE separately if you need a non-blocking change. Never drop a money-safety invariant.

Trigger function takes row-level lock on accounts row (SELECT FOR UPDATE implicit in UPDATE) inside the inserter's transaction. For a hot account, all concurrent payments serialize on this row lock — same hot-row pattern as a global counter. Throughput collapses for high-velocity accounts.

Architectural anti-pattern: derived data (account.balance_cents) is being computed eagerly via trigger when it should be derived lazily from ledger_entries. The 'event sourcing' pattern with materialized snapshots is industry-standard for financial ledgers. Recompute balance from ledger in a separate consistency check; do not embed in the write path.
